mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% YoY, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the CPIAUCSL index below 0.1% signals disinflation that removes rate hike fears, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM growth falls below 0.1%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening economic activity that reduces corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines show no predictive power for negative 60-day returns, with a rolling 2-year Rank IC consistently below -0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand strength that supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day return direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the industrial production index during periods of elevated interest rates signals capacity constraints that compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, with directional accuracy below 45% over a 3-year sample.
